sources.name: wiki
sources.title: Engineering Wiki
sources.description: Canonical home for architecture decisions (ADRs), runbooks, and process.
sources.instructions:
Capabilities: search, get-content — via helper scripts in ./source-scripts
(an example of wrapping an external system as small CLI tools).
- Search: `./source-scripts/wiki-search "<query>"` → prints matching "<id>\t<title>" lines.
- Get content: `./source-scripts/wiki-get-article <id>` → prints the article content.
- Cite articles by title + id. The guide summarises these; never contradict them.
- (Demo: the scripts grep local sample files under sources/docs/wiki; in a real
  project they'd call the wiki's API with the same input/output contract.)

sources.name: platform
sources.title: platform
sources.description: The backend API monorepo (Node + TypeScript, Postgres) — authoritative for how the system behaves.
sources.instructions: Authoritative for how the system actually behaves (the code is the source of truth). - Local checkout: `sources/code/platform` — grep/read it to verify behavior. - Freshness: confirm the clone is current (`git -C sources/code/platform log -1 --format=%cr`) before trusting an absence. - Link in the guide as ; deep references resolve under https://github.com/demo-co/platform. Repo-root links only.

sources.title: web
sources.description: The frontend monorepo (Next.js apps + shared packages) — authoritative for what the product looks like.
sources.instructions: Authoritative for how the system actually behaves (the code is the source of truth). - Local checkout: `sources/code/web` — grep/read it to verify behavior. - Freshness: confirm the clone is current (`git -C sources/code/web log -1 --format=%cr`) before trusting an absence. - Link in the guide as ; deep references resolve under https://github.com/demo-co/web. sources.name: help-center
sources.title: Help Center
sources.description: Customer-facing help articles (an Astro content repo).
sources.instructions:
Authoritative for product-facing behavior (help/marketing content).
- Local checkout: `sources/docs/help-center` — search the article source (e.g. Astro/Markdown content).
- Cite the published article URL under https://help.demo.co.
- On conflict: update the help article first, then the guide.
